title: camissonia specicola subsp. hesperia
intro: Camissonia specicola subsp. hesperia, also known as the desert gold poppy, is a subspecies of the annual flowering plant Camissonia specicola, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used by Native American tribes for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Camissonia specicola subsp. hesperia include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
